reasoning: The baseline to beat is the value on 2025-12-21, which will correspond to the most recent weekly publication for Wednesday 2025-12-17 (89.0). This 89.0 value is already quite elevated compared to the average of 20-50 seen in mid-to-late 2025. Typically, there is a massive year-end spike in Central Bank Liquidity Swaps as foreign banks secure USD around the turn of the year (e.g., 2024-12-25 saw a value of 1121.0). Therefore, the value for the week encompassing 2025-12-28 is highly likely to exceed 89.0. By January 2026, the year-end effects should subside, and the swap usage normally drops significantly, meaning it will likely fall below the 89.0 threshold (although there's occasional residual demand, leading to a sub-0.5 probability). For March 2026, we expect the value to revert fully to baseline levels (usually well below 89), but forecast uncertainty pushes the probability closer to 0.5.
